keys: Save the sheet - compile-on-save keeps its generated script fresh (Ctrl+S).
Save, then play the scene that uses this sheet's script.
Add a condition to the selected event (C).
Add an action to the selected event (A).
Add a GDScript block to the selected event - the deliberate 'drop to code' escape hatch. Opens the code editor immediately.
Beginner-friendly view: hides the advanced/code entries (GDScript blocks, sub-conditions, pick filters, signals/enums). Everything still works when off.
Toggle the generated-GDScript panel - the sheet's honest compiled output, side by side.
Theme for this sheet (Load/Reload and the Theme Editor live in View)
Create, open, save, and configure this event sheet.
Panels, multi-view panes, theme, live values, and zoom.
Debug tools, validation, import, and project workflow.
Teach a Verb - Share Published Verbs
Hide the advanced/code entries (GDScript blocks, sub-conditions, pick filters, match, signals/enums) from the right-click menus. Everything still works in Expert mode.
Show/hide the generated-GDScript panel beside the sheet.
Show/hide the trailing "+ Add event…" rows. Turn off for a cleaner, calmer sheet.
Show/hide a second synchronized view of this sheet, side by side.
Pop the sheet view out into its own window / bring it back.
Link/unlink scrolling between the split views.
Lint every ƒx expression + GDScript block; flag the offending rows and jump to the first.
Toggle breakpoint emission: debug-compiled sheets pause at rows with breakpoints.
Toggle Live Values: running sheets stream their variables here (editable).
Double-click anywhere - or press E - to add your first event.
Double-click anywhere - or press E - to add an event that drives the Node this attaches to.
Create one to start building - a menu of ready-made starters opens.
Add the signals and functions every sheet in the project should reach (score, game state, an event bus).
Tip: the picker understands plain language. Try typing "every tick".
Tip: Tools > Welcome… has a playable showcase and a 2-minute tour.
Tip: publish a function to the picker and every sheet in the project can call it.

A coloured section label drawn above this property in the Inspector.

Editor decor only - plain comment in the code.
A whole number (no decimals) - stored as an int. Unticked stores a float.
Any type - untyped (advanced; prefer a specific type when you can).

Edit an Array/Dictionary's items one per line instead of typing a literal.
Event-sheet-style quick add: type an event/condition/action (event-sheet phrasing works) plus optional parameter values, press Enter.
Every sheet function at a glance (✦ = published as an ACE). Click to expand or collapse.
How this variable's field LOOKS in the Inspector - pickers, flags, layer grids... Plain fields need no choice here.
Inspector grouping, conditional show/lock, an on-changed callback, clamp, read-only - for variables that reference other variables or functions.

Make this sheet's published verbs (its exposed ƒ functions) available in EVERY sheet's picker, node-targeted at $<ClassName>. Extract actions to a function first (right-click an event), then teach it here.
No alpha (Color: solid RGB, no transparency)

On: a designer can tweak this per-instance in the Inspector (@export var).
Off: internal script state - a plain private var (the default for a new variable).

On: compiles to @onready var - the Default is a GDScript EXPRESSION evaluated when the node enters the tree (e.g. $Player, get_node("UI/Score")), not a literal.
Use it to grab a node reference once the scene is ready.

One column per entry: name:type (String, int, float, bool, color for a swatch, or enum(a|b|c) for a dropdown).
Each Array element becomes a row with these cells.
Optional Inspector polish for exported globals - everything compiles to plain Godot annotations.
Pick the Inspector look from picture tiles instead of the list.

Renders a small button with this field that calls the named sheet function.
Needs a @tool sheet to act in-editor. Label optional (defaults to the function name).
Required (warn in the Inspector while unset)

Scaffold a ready-to-edit behaviour script in res://eventsheet_addons/ - its signals become triggers, methods become actions/conditions, and @export vars become properties, all auto-discovered as custom ACEs.
See the WHOLE sheet's Inspector as one live view - every exported variable with its decor, grouping, and widget, exactly as Godot will show it.
Shown after the number in the Inspector (suffix:px).

Shows a red warning above the field until a value is assigned
(a Resource left empty, a String left blank). Editor-only.
Start editing this sheet here. From then on, Save (Ctrl+S) updates the file - or use Save As… to keep the original and save a copy.

Swap the Inspector field for a richer drawer (dial, swatches, curve…).
Graceful: a plain field without the editor plugin (parity preserved).

The Inspector calls this function while the property is edited and shows
the returned message above the field. Needs a @tool sheet to run in-editor.
The slider stops at max but typing a bigger number is allowed (or_greater).
The variable's type. For a node reference, type the node's class (Sprite2D, Label, Area2D…) or leave Variant.
Turn the MCP server (AI-assistant tools) on/off. When off, connected AI clients see no tools and can't read or change your sheets. Takes effect live - no reconnect needed.
Write this sheet's plain, standalone GDScript to a file you own. No plugin dependency - proof you can leave the addon anytime.
comma-separated, e.g. easy, normal, hard (String only)
function and button label, e.g. reroll_stats Reroll
grey hint shown when the field is empty
nested section under the group (e.g. Melee)
note panel above (e.g. Shared resource - edits affect every user.)

type - e.g. Node2D, Label, Sprite2D (or Variant for any)
what this variable is for - shown as its description in the Inspector
Documentation shown above the function (## …). BBCode like [b]bold[/b] is allowed.
The name of the setter's incoming-value parameter (defaults to `value`).
The statements that run when this variable is assigned (`set(value):`). Use `value` for the incoming value. Leave blank for no setter.
The statements that run when this variable is read (`get:`), ending in `return …`. Leave blank for no getter.
setter body, e.g.  health = clampi(value, 0, max_health)

A list of text entries (Array[String]) - names, lines, tags.

A list that can hold anything (mixed types allowed).
Fixed width of the object-name column in the actions lane (0 = text flows after each name). Also set by dragging the name/text gap in a row.
Fixed width of the object-name column in the conditions lane (0 = text flows after each name). Also set by dragging the name/text gap in a row.
Height of a group header bar, in pixels - double an event row by default; lower it for the classic slim bar.
Make your own data asset in three questions: name one entry, list its columns, done - a Resource whose Inspector is a fill-in table, saved as .tres files designers edit.
Named anything (by text key) - a flexible data bag.
Named numbers (by text key) - e.g. volumes["music"] = 0.8.
Named slots holding anything: look values up by a key.
Named text (by text key) - e.g. lines["greeting"].
Named whole numbers (by text key) - e.g. costs["sword"] = 10.
Start an editor-side tool sheet: its events run INSIDE the editor (script editor > File > Run), never in the game - batch renames, scene checks, one-click chores.
Switch the EventSheets editor language. Drop a translation CSV into addons/eventsheet/translations/ and it appears here automatically.

Makes your own data asset: a Resource with a table designers fill in the Inspector and save as .tres files - loot tables, dialogue lines, wave plans, anything that is rows of data. No annotations to learn; describe the rows and the sheet is generated.

One column per line. Plain words make a text column; add ": float", ": int", or ": bool" for numbers and toggles; list choices with | for a dropdown.

name
kind: coin|gem|key
weight: float
Warn in the Inspector until the table has rows (required)
Add a validation check (a function warning about bad data, live)
Creates a ready-to-edit validate function: it runs while the table is edited in the Inspector and shows any warning it returns above the field. Add your own rules as condition rows.
Show this function as a one-click button in the Inspector, labelled with this text. Pressing the button runs the function's rows - great for editor chores like re-baking, refilling, or validating.
Plain GDScript - anything valid in an expression works here. Ctrl+Space completes sheet variables/functions and host members. Alt+Enter grows the box for a long expression (and shrinks it back).
Find Expressions - open the expressions dictionary (floating: double-click inserts, stays open until you close it)
Double-click to insert at the cursor - the dictionary stays open so results chain. You can also just type in the field: the same expressions autocomplete as you go.
Name a counter that runs 0, 1, 2... each pass - Construct's loopindex. Read it with the Loop Index expression; give nested loops distinct names and read an outer one with Loop Index Of.
Write one value per line (multi-line enum)
Off: enum State { IDLE, RUN }. On: each value on its own tab-indented line - the usual shape for long enums with explicit numbers.

Sheets aren't only for gameplay: Sheet > New Custom Resource… asks three plain questions (what one entry is called, what columns it has, whether it's required) and builds a data asset whose Inspector is a fill-in table - loot tables, dialogue lines, wave plans. Designers fill rows and save .tres variants; your sheets load them. And Sheet > New Editor Tool… makes one-click editor chores the same way.
Peek at Sheet > New Custom Resource… (Esc closes it without creating anything).
An On <Signal> event is added to the node's sheet; its actions run when the signal fires.
Connected %s - add actions to the new event.
Open the EventSheet workspace to connect signals.
Wrap the selection in [color=...] (edit the hex after)
